question: What concrete whole-boundary kernel shape can make recompute plus group-aware dKV accumulation materialize, after rejecting late dKV-only, HSK slab, group-owned, and scalar segment reducers?
inputs: attn_sink/delta as needed for sink gradients
must_not_materialize: selected-row dKV partial slab whose size is O(S*K*D)

evidence_closing_bad_branches.branch: late TileLang dKV drop-in after PyTorch materialization
evidence_closing_bad_branches.reason: Kernel-only dKV wins did not materialize e2e because TorchTitan had already paid selected_kv/probs/grad_logits bridge cost.
Correct after no-merge fix, but HSK export was about 34.8 ms and the branch was non-durable e2e.
Avoided HSK slabs and reduced writes, but group ownership reloaded/recomputed q/dO too often and destroyed query-major reuse.
Modeled 23.8x write reduction still timed at 116.04 ms because each segment/D-block CTA serialized too much contribution work.

current_query_major_kernel_read.good_property: Recomputes QK/probs/grad_logits inside the kernel and keeps q/dO reuse for one query token while producing dQ.
current_query_major_kernel_read.bad_property: The CTA sees only one token, so the dKV epilogue cannot reduce many tokens that select the same compressed group before global memory. It emits raw tl.atomic_add for safe_idx x D chunks.

next_kernel_contract.name: query_major_token_tile_nohsk_compact_reducer
next_kernel_contract.owner: token tile x head block x selected window, with optional group panel metadata from prepacked LI route
producer_loop: Load q and dO for a token tile and head block once into registers/shared memory.
For each selected window, gather compressed/shared KV rows through LI indices.
Recompute qk, probabilities, grad_probs, softmax row term, and scaled dP inside the kernel.
Produce dQ with the same query-major accumulation path.
Produce dKV contributions with MFMA/dot math while q/dO are still hot.
group_aware_epilogue: Map each contribution row (token, selected_slot) to compressed group g before any global write.
Within the token tile, perform lane/warp/block segmented reduction keyed by g and D block.
Emit one reduced atomic or compact partial row per active (group, D block) per producer tile, not one write per (token, selected_slot, D).
If compact partial rows are emitted, use the already-cheap final fold/reduce path; do not emit an O(S*K*D) slab.
next_kernel_contract.why_this_can_be_group_aware: Unlike the current one-token query-major Triton CTA, the token-tile producer sees several tokens' selected groups at once, so it can find repeated compressed groups inside the tile and reduce them before HBM. Unlike group-owned reducers, it still keeps q/dO hot across the selected sweep instead of reloading q/dO for every group.
next_kernel_contract.first_latency_gate: Beat the 42.54 ms no-HSK direct atomic whole-boundary path at S4096, then beat the 8.0-8.8 ms compressed dKV atomic slice before an e2e TFLOPs A/B.
next_kernel_contract.promotion_gate: Only promote after a clean 4x or 8x TorchTitan S4096 e2e run shows sustained late-step TFLOPs and memory improvement.

implementation_note: This is not a final performance result. It is the concrete replacement contract for the sparse-MLA backward work after today’s HIP tile-segment scalar reducer rejection.
